primary_method.name: SOTP
primary_method.reasoning: NVDA has four distinct economic segments with materially different growth, margins, risk, and appropriate valuation methods. Data Center is a high-growth platform (90% of revenue, 68% YoY growth) where exit-multiple is the most efficient method. Gaming and Professional Visualization are mature near-monopoly annuities valued at current earnings x justified multiple. Automotive is an early-stage growth option valued via exit-multiple at a 2030 normalization. Blending all four into one DCF or one multiple would conflate irreconcilably different risk and growth profiles.

segments.name: Data Center
segments.reasoning: High-growth segment (FY2026 $193.74B, +68% YoY; FY2027 tracking $350B). EXIT-MULTIPLE is the most efficient method: build NOPAT to normalization year FY2030 (T=4 years from base year FY2026), apply steady-state Gordon multiple, discount at DC segment WACC. This isolates the only real question -- how big does DC earnings get at normalization -- and avoids g>r blow-up in a forced perpetual DCF during the ramp years. The normalization year is FY2030 per Theia demand model (NVDA_demand.json), where DC revenue reaches $560B base and the CAGR decelerates from 30% to ~10% as the AI capex cycle matures.

derivation.nopat_T_derivation: DC revenue FY2030 base $560B (NVDA_demand.json scenarios.base.fy2030_revenue_dc_usd_bn) x 65% EBIT margin x (1 - 8% cash tax rate) = $560B x 0.65 x 0.92 = $335.0B. EBIT margin of 65% is calibrated to Q1 FY2027 actuals: GM 74.9% (NVDA_market_facts.json latest_earnings.gross_margin_gaap_pct), opex ~9% of revenue (guidance $8.3B non-GAAP on $91B Q2 guide), operating margin ~65-66%. Cash tax rate 8% is below the GAAP provision (~7.9% implied FY2026) but accounts for forward R&D credit normalization; below 15% forward consensus because NVDA's SBC deductions structurally hold cash taxes below GAAP provision.
derivation.nopat_path_derivation: Path from FY2027 to FY2030 applying 65% EBIT margin x 0.92 to DC revenues: FY2027 $350B = $209.3B NOPAT; FY2028 $442B = $264.3B; FY2029 $510B = $305.1B; FY2030 $560B = $335.0B. FY2027 revenue anchored to Q1 actual $75.2B + Q2 guide $83.7B + H2 estimate = $350B (NVDA_demand.json). FY2028 anchored to post-Q1 analyst consensus $480B total x 92% DC mix = $442B (NVDA_demand.json calibration note). FY2029-FY2030 derived from crux wallet-share x pool identity in NVDA_demand.json.
derivation.r_derivation: CAPM: Ke = rf + beta x ERP. rf = 4.4% (10-yr UST at valuation date 2026-06-20). Beta = 1.05 fundamental (NOT the regression beta of 2.2 from NVDA_databundle.json price.beta, which is rally-inflated and explicitly rejected per CONTRACT §2.3). Fundamental beta rationale: DC segment has (a) customer concentration risk (top 4 = 61% of Q3 FY2026 revenue per NVDA_industry.json), (b) hardware capex cyclicality risk, offset by (c) $119B manufacturing/supply commitments providing near-term de-risking (NVDA_crux.json contracted_horizon), (d) CUDA ecosystem moat giving structural durability (NVDA_industry.json moat). Beta 1.05 is above a software annuity (0.7-0.8) to reflect hardware cyclicality and concentration, but well below the rally regression (2.2). ERP = 5.5% (consensus US ERP). Ke = 4.4% + 1.05 x 5.5% = 4.4% + 5.775% = 10.175%, rounded to 10.2%. NVDA is essentially debt-free (net debt $0.44B vs $5.1T market cap), so WACC = Ke = 10.2%.
derivation.g_lt_derivation: 5.5% long-run growth post-normalization for DC. Bloomberg Intelligence projects AI accelerator market at 16% CAGR to $604B by 2033 (NVDA_demand.json driver_ceilings). Post-2033 deceleration toward nominal GDP + AI structural premium. Nominal GDP growth ~4-5%; AI infrastructure adds 1-2% structural premium (AI penetration across all industries continues beyond 2030 as inference scales and agentic AI expands). 5.5% g_LT reflects this deceleration without assuming AI compute becomes a pure commodity. g_LT < r (5.5% < 10.2%), so Gordon formula is valid.
derivation.roic_derivation: 55% steady-state incremental ROIC. NVDA's observed ROIC is extraordinary (ROE 76.3% per NVDA_databundle.json ratios; asset-light fabless model). At normalization, competition (AMD at 13-15% merchant share per NVDA_crux.json base, ASIC pressure) caps ROIC below current levels. 55% is conservative vs observed but above typical semiconductor ROIC (Intel ~10%, Qualcomm ~30%, Broadcom ~40%), crediting CUDA ecosystem stickiness. Note: ROIC is second-order in this model -- changing ROIC 45% to 55% moves the exit multiple only 0.5x and the share value ~$12, confirming ROIC is not the call's hinge.
derivation.interim_fcf_pv_derivation: PV of DC segment FCF in years 1-3 (FY2027-FY2029), using 45% FCF margin on DC revenue at 10.2% discount rate. Validation: FY2026 blended FCF margin was 44.8% (NVDA_databundle.json ratios.fcf_margin_pct); DC-specific margin is slightly above blended (lower capex intensity than gaming). 45% is conservative to account for working capital build during growth ramp (NVDA has $119B supply commitments impacting cash). FCF per year: FY2027 $350B x 45% = $157.5B / 1.102 = $142.9B; FY2028 $442B x 45% = $198.9B / 1.102^2 = $163.7B; FY2029 $510B x 45% = $229.5B / 1.102^3 = $171.3B. Total PV: $477.9B, rounded to $478B.

segments.name: Gaming
segments.reasoning: Mature-annuity segment. NVDA holds 94-95% discrete GPU market share (Jon Peddie Research, NVDA_industry.json) with no credible competitive threat. RTX 50 series drives ASP lift offsetting unit pressure from tariff headwinds. Growth is mid-single-digit; the segment is at steady state. Current earnings x justified multiple is the most efficient method.

multiple_inputs.reasoning: Gaming FY2027 EBIT: $16.5B x 37% EBIT margin = $6.1B. EBIT margin 37%: Gaming GM ~55% (NVDA_industry.json segments), lower than DC due to hardware and AIB distribution; EBIT margin is lower than GM by R&D allocated to gaming (~12% of gaming revenue) + SG&A. Justified multiple = Gordon output: g_LT=4.0% (mid-single-digit per Theia; RTX 50 AI features drive ASP, but AIB market -10% in 2026 per Jon Peddie), ROIC=40%, r=9.0%. Multiple = (1-0.04/0.40)/(0.09-0.04) = 0.90/0.05 = 18.0x. r=9.0%: Ke = 4.4% + 0.85 x 5.5% = 9.075%; beta 0.85 for a near-monopoly mature consumer segment (lower fundamental risk than DC, stable gaming upgrade cycle, no competing platform). Cross-check: mature consumer tech with 90%+ market share and stable FCF trades at 15-20x EBIT; 18x is at center.

segments.name: Professional Visualization
segments.reasoning: Mature-annuity near-monopoly. NVDA has >90% workstation GPU share (NVDA_industry.json addressable_market). TAM $3.5B growing at ~8% CAGR (Verified Market Reports). RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell drives AI workstation demand. Current earnings x justified multiple is the most efficient method.

multiple_inputs.reasoning: ProViz FY2027 EBIT: $3.5B x 50% EBIT margin = $1.75B. EBIT margin 50%: GM ~65% (NVDA_industry.json segments), lower than DC but premium workstation pricing; EBIT after allocated R&D and SG&A. g_LT=5.0% (above Gaming: TAM growing 8% CAGR, AI workstation demand drives refresh), ROIC=45%, r=8.5%. Gordon multiple = (1-0.05/0.45)/(0.085-0.05) = 0.889/0.035 = 25.4x. Capped to 23x for conservatism vs hardware comps: professional software/hardware near-monopoly (comparable to workstation software at 25-30x, but ProViz has hardware economics). r=8.5%: Ke = 4.4% + 0.75 x 5.5% = 8.525%; beta 0.75 for a near-monopoly enterprise segment with very low substitution risk.

segments.name: Automotive
segments.reasoning: Early-stage high-growth. NVIDIA DRIVE Thor dominates new design wins; $14B design win pipeline through 2028 (NVDA_industry.json). Revenue recognition is 2-4 years lagged. This is a real but small call option; exit-multiple at FY2030 normalization appropriate. Immaterial to total SOTP (<1% contribution) but included for completeness.

derivation.nopat_T_derivation: Automotive revenue FY2030 estimated $8B (continuing Theia's ramp: $6B FY2029 + $2B growth from further design win programs reaching production). EBIT margin 30%: Automotive ADAS/AV compute has lower margins than DC due to OEM integration, custom software requirements, and competitive pressure from Mobileye/Qualcomm Snapdragon Ride. NOPAT = $8B x 30% x 85% (1-15% tax) = $2.04B.
derivation.r_derivation: r=11%: Ke = 4.4% + 1.2 x 5.5% = 11.0%. Beta 1.2: Automotive design win cycles are lumpy (2-3 year programs), OEM-dependent, and have more execution risk than DC. Higher than DC beta despite smaller segment because of program-level binary risk.
derivation.g_lt_derivation: 8.0% long-run: automotive AV/ADAS compute is genuinely early-stage; TAM grows from $3B to $12B+ by 2030 (NVDA_industry.json). Post-2030 growth remains elevated as AV penetration rises. 8% is modest vs the TAM trajectory.
derivation.roic_derivation: 30%: lower than DC because automotive requires more hardware integration, co-engineering with OEMs, and longer design cycles that tie up R&D investment.

cross_check.name: NTM P/E vs non-GAAP EPS
cross_check.reasoning: Cross-check: NVDA at $207 base fair value vs $9.34 FY2027 non-GAAP EPS (Pheme corrected). Justified P/E = $207 / $9.34 = 22.2x. Current market P/E on non-GAAP FY2027 = $210.69 / $9.34 = 22.6x. Our base implies the stock is FAIRLY VALUED at essentially the same P/E -- the market has it right at $210, and any upside comes from FY2028+ ramp materializing. Peer check: AMD at 41x NTM P/E (NVDA_databundle.json peers) is more expensive, reflecting AMD's earlier stage of AI ramp. AVGO at 21x is the most comparable mature high-quality semi at lower growth. NVDA at 22-23x NTM non-GAAP for 85% revenue growth (Q1 FY2027 vs Q1 FY2026) is genuinely inexpensive on a PEG basis (PEG = 0.65 per bundle).

earnings_bridge.notes: Owner FCF = reported FCF ($96.68B) - SBC ($4.50B net; SBC estimated ~2% of revenue at $216B revenue, consistent with Theia note of 8-10% declining as revenue scales and SBC is reclassified into non-GAAP starting Q1 FY2027). Buybacks of $40.09B MORE than offset $4.50B SBC, so net share count is declining; owner FCF is charged for SBC as a real comp cost. The FY2026 owner FCF of $92.18B validates the model: at FY2027 DC mix (92.1%), the FCF from DC alone at 45% FCF margin on $350B = $157.5B, growing from the $87B DC FCF implied in FY2026 ($96.68B blended x 90.3% DC revenue share = $87.3B DC FCF). The ramp trajectory is consistent. SBC reclassification note: per NVDA_market_facts.json eps_ntm_artifact_check, NVDA starting Q1 FY2027 includes SBC in non-GAAP, narrowing the GAAP/non-GAAP gap. This is material for forward earnings comparisons.
earnings_bridge.base_year_validation: FY2026: EBIT $130.39B (60.4% margin) - cash taxes ~$10.3B (7.9% effective) = NOPAT $120.1B. Plus D&A $14.16B - capex $6.04B - WC change (estimated -$17B from ramp-year AR/inventory) = approx CFO $102.72B (actual). CFO plus SBC addback ($4.5B non-cash) = $107.2B vs actual $102.72B; difference attributable to WC and other. The model is internally consistent within $5B (<5% of CFO).

valuation_anchor._note: Using FY2027 non-GAAP EPS consensus of $9.34 per NVDA_market_facts.json eps_ntm_artifact_check.fy2027_nongaap_consensus for the P/E dual. The bundle's blended NTM EPS of $12.73 is FLAGGED as an annualization artifact (per Pheme) and is not used.

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.probability_reasoning: Probability weighting is NOT applied (METHODS.md: no probability-weighted EV; bull and bear are the honest range). Evidence quality: MEDIUM per NVDA_crux.json bear.evidence_quality -- ASIC acceleration well-evidenced, AMD ROCm gap closure timing speculative.
scenarios.reasoning: Bear scenario corresponds to NVDA_demand.json scenarios.bear where DC FY2030 = $460B. ASIC revenue share reaches 29-32% by FY2029 (Broadcom $100B+ sustaining, AMD ROCm at 80% CUDA coverage). NOPAT at T: $460B x 0.65 x 0.92 = $275.1B. All other inputs unchanged (base WACC 10.2%, g_LT 5.5%, ROIC 55%). Exit multiple 19.15x. EV at T = $275.1B x 19.15 = $5,268B. PV = $5,268 / 1.4756 = $3,570B. Interim FCF PV (DC bear: $340B FY2027 x 45% = $153B / 1.102 = $138.8B; $390B FY2028 x 45% = $175.5B / 1.102^2 = $143.6B; $430B FY2029 x 45% = $193.5B / 1.102^3 = $143.2B; total $425.6B). DC EV bear = $3,570 + $426 = $3,996B. Plus other segments $183B. Total equity = $3,996 + $183 - $0.44 = $4,178.6B. Per share: $4,178.6 / 24.221 = $172.53.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.probability_reasoning: The base IS the argued most-likely -- not a midpoint. Evidence: contracted FY2027 ($95B supply commitments), anchored FY2028 (consensus $480B total), extrapolated FY2029-FY2030 from ASIC share trajectory at moderate pace. Evidence quality: HIGH FY2027, MEDIUM FY2028-FY2030.
scenarios.reasoning: Base scenario per NVDA_demand.json scenarios.base. DC FY2030 = $560B. NOPAT at T = $335B. All inputs as stated in exit_inputs. This is the SOTP primary output = $207/share.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.probability_reasoning: Evidence quality: MEDIUM per NVDA_crux.json bull.evidence_quality -- Vera Rubin 10x cost-per-token claim is management guidance, not independently verified at scale.
scenarios.reasoning: Bull scenario corresponds to NVDA_demand.json scenarios.bull where DC FY2030 = $603B (ceiling-capped at $900B AI chip pool). NOPAT at T: $603B x 0.65 x 0.92 = $360.6B. Exit multiple 19.15x. EV at T = $360.6B x 19.15 = $6,906B. PV = $6,906 / 1.4756 = $4,680B. Interim FCF PV (DC bull: $360B FY2027 x 45% = $162B / 1.102 = $147.0B; $490B FY2028 x 45% = $220.5B / 1.102^2 = $180.5B; $590B FY2029 x 45% = $265.5B / 1.102^3 = $196.6B; total $524.1B). DC EV bull = $4,680 + $524 = $5,204B. Plus other segments $183B. Total equity = $5,204 + $183 - $0.44 = $5,386.6B. Per share: $5,386.6 / 24.221 = $222.39.

blending_logic: Pure SOTP — sum of segment EVs less parent net debt
